incident.title: Kerberoasting — svc-jenkins TGS Hash Capture and Crack
incident.outcome: svc-jenkins password cracked. WinRM shell obtained on DC01 as svc-jenkins.

kill_chain.name: SPN Enumeration
kill_chain.result: svc-jenkins identified with SPN HTTP/jenkins.corp.local. PasswordLastSet 2026-03-26. LastLogon never.
kill_chain.detection_note: LDAP query — no Security event log entry generated. VIS-005.

kill_chain.name: TGS Ticket Request
kill_chain.detection_note: EC4768 (TGT) + EC4769 (TGS) at 18:01:36 — 9ms apart. Client_Address=::ffff:10.10.10.99. Ticket_Encryption_Type=0x17.

kill_chain.name: Offline Hash Cracking
kill_chain.result: Service@2025! recovered. Cracked instantly from targeted wordlist.
kill_chain.detection_note: Offline operation — no logging system can detect this.

kill_chain.name: WinRM Access as svc-jenkins
kill_chain.result: Shell obtained on DC01 as svc-jenkins. *Evil-WinRM* PS C:\>

splunk_evidence.query: index=windows EventCode=4769 Ticket_Encryption_Type=0x17 | where NOT match(Service_Name, "\\$$") | stats count by Account_Name, Service_Name, Client_Address
index=windows | search src_ip="::ffff:10.10.10.99" OR Client_Address="::ffff:10.10.10.99" | stats count by EventCode, Account_Name, Service_Name
splunk_evidence.result: EC4768 jsmith krbtgt (1), EC4769 jsmith@CORP.LOCAL svc-jenkins (1)

splunk_evidence.query: index=windows | search Client_Address="::ffff:10.10.10.99" | table _time, EventCode, Account_Name, Service_Name, Ticket_Encryption_Type | sort _time
splunk_evidence.result: 18:01:36.724 EC4768 jsmith krbtgt 0x17 | 18:01:36.733 EC4769 jsmith@CORP.LOCAL svc-jenkins 0x17

detections.name: RC4 TGS Request for Service Account
detections.filter: Ticket_Encryption_Type=0x17 AND Service_Name not ending in $

detections.name: TGT + TGS in same second from non-domain host
detections.filter: 9ms gap, same Client_Address, Linux source IP

detections.name: Service account WinRM login from Linux host

visibility_gaps.description: SPN enumeration via LDAP is completely silent
visibility_gaps.impact: Attacker identifies all Kerberoastable accounts with zero detection
visibility_gaps.fix: Enable LDAP audit logging or deploy honeypot SPNs

recommendations: Implement Group Managed Service Accounts (gMSA) for svc-jenkins — auto-rotating 240-char passwords
Set msDS-SupportedEncryptionTypes to AES only on all service accounts — disable RC4
Deploy honeypot SPN account — any TGS request = immediate critical alert
Audit all service accounts for SPN registrations and enforce strong passwords
Investigate how jsmith credentials were obtained (reference ATK-001)
